stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz Deal Set to Close This Week
stories.sub: Interim agreement would reopen critical shipping lane after months of Iranian blockade
stories.summary: An interim accord to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is expected to be signed by week's end. The deal follows months of escalating tensions that sent oil prices surging. President Trump publicly claimed credit for brokering the agreement at a celebratory White House event.

stories.headline: Russia Strikes Kyiv Monastery, Kills 5 Rescue Workers in Kharkiv
stories.sub: Landmark Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery set ablaze in overnight bombardment
stories.summary: A large-scale Russian assault overnight killed five rescue workers in Kharkiv and wounded 20 in Kyiv. The strike sparked a fire at the Monastery of Caves, one of Ukraine's most significant religious and cultural landmarks. Apartment buildings across both cities were also set ablaze.

stories.headline: Crown Princess's Son Gets 4 Years for Sexual Assault
stories.sub: Marius Borg Hoiby convicted on two of four rape charges in Oslo court
stories.summary: Marius Borg Hoiby, son of Crown Princess Mette-Marit of Norway, was sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty on two counts of rape. The ruling follows a prolonged trial that drew intense public attention in Norway.

stories.headline: Carney Heads to France for G7 as Iran Deal Dominates Agenda
stories.sub: PM to meet allies as Ottawa also tables privacy and clean water legislation this week
stories.summary: Prime Minister Carney is travelling to the G7 summit in France as the Iran interim deal reshapes alliance dynamics. Back home, the government is introducing privacy reform and clean drinking water legislation.

stories.headline: Drone Seizures Near World Cup Venues as Security Tightens Across City
stories.sub: Toronto Police confirm multiple unauthorized drones intercepted since festivities began
stories.summary: Toronto Police have seized several unauthorized drones near World Cup venues since the tournament's opening events began last week. Officers are expected to brief media Tuesday on arrests tied to multiple investigations in the city.

stories.headline: Province Commits $198M to Ontario Place Parking Garage
stories.sub: Critics question spending priority as transit gaps persist across the city
stories.summary: The provincial government announced $198 million for a new parking facility at Ontario Place, drawing immediate criticism from transit advocates who argue the funds should address chronic gaps in the TTC and regional transit network.

stories.headline: Man Sentenced to 4.5 Years for 2023 East End Shooting Death
stories.sub: Sentence handed down Monday in city's east end killing
stories.summary: A Toronto man received a four-and-a-half-year prison sentence for his role in the 2023 fatal shooting in the city's east end. The case was among several high-profile Toronto violence cases to move through the courts this spring.

stories.headline: Olivia Chow Declares Re-election Bid; Bradford Mounts Challenge
stories.sub: October 26 municipal vote shaping up as two-horse race for mayor
stories.summary: Mayor Olivia Chow formally declared her candidacy for the October 26 municipal election. City Councillor Brad Bradford has registered as a challenger. The nomination period runs until August 21.

stories.headline: Iran Deal: What's in the Interim Agreement and What Comes Next
stories.sub: Analysts say temporary Hormuz reopening leaves core nuclear disputes unresolved
stories.summary: The emerging interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz would allow commercial shipping to resume while broader negotiations continue. Experts caution the arrangement is fragile and no framework has been established for permanent resolution.

stories.headline: Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Fire: What Was Damaged at the Thousand-Year-Old Site
stories.sub: UNESCO-listed monastery has survived wars and Soviet-era suppression
stories.summary: The overnight fire at the Monastery of Caves, a UNESCO World Heritage site dating to 1051, destroyed portions of the historic Dormition Cathedral. Church officials and Ukrainian cultural authorities have begun assessing damage.

stories.headline: Royal Family in Crisis After Conviction of Crown Princess's Son
stories.sub: Four-year sentence follows trial that transfixed Norway for months
stories.summary: The Norwegian royal family faces sustained public scrutiny following the conviction of Marius Borg Hoiby. Crown Princess Mette-Marit issued a brief statement expressing sorrow. Commentators note the monarchy's approval ratings have fallen.

stories.headline: Cape Verde Stuns Spain; Saudi Arabia Holds Uruguay to Shock Draws
stories.sub: Day 5 rewrites early odds as tournament favourites drop points across the board
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Vozinha became an overnight sensation Monday after Cape Verde held Spain to a 0-0 draw. Saudi Arabia also earned a hard-fought draw against Uruguay. Germany remains the standout performer after their 7-1 demolition of Curacao.

stories.headline: Today's Fixtures: France, Argentina, Norway All in Action
stories.sub: France vs. Senegal leads Day 6; Argentina face Algeria; Austria take on Jordan
stories.summary: Tuesday's four-match slate opens with France against Senegal at MetLife Stadium at 3 PM local. Norway face Iraq, Argentina take on Algeria, and Austria close out the night against Jordan.

stories.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Win the Stanley Cup
stories.sub: Canes claim the title in a series that went the distance
stories.summary: The Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions. The title capped a long playoff run and ends a lengthy championship drought for the franchise. Details on the celebration plans are still emerging Tuesday morning.

stories.headline: Anthropic Proposes Global AI Pause as Compute Costs Drive IPO Talk
stories.sub: President Daniela Amodei says rising infrastructure costs make public offering increasingly likely
stories.summary: Anthropic published what it called its most significant safety paper of the year: a proposal for a coordinated global AI development pause. Separately, president Daniela Amodei explained that surging compute costs are pushing the company toward an IPO.

stories.headline: Hormuz Deal Drives Oil Price Drop in Early Trading
stories.sub: Brent crude falls sharply on news of interim agreement
stories.summary: Oil markets moved decisively on news of the Hormuz interim deal, with Brent crude falling in early European trading. Traders caution the move may reverse if the agreement fails to materialize.

stories.headline: The Hormuz Deal Is Good News. Don't Let Trump Own the Narrative.
stories.sub: Credit where it's due, but the hard work of durable peace in the Gulf is still ahead
stories.summary: An interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is genuinely welcome. But framing it as a Trump diplomatic triumph misreads the underlying dynamics. Iran's economic pain and regional exhaustion drove this agreement as much as any White House positioning.

stories.headline: Ontario Place Parking Garage Is Everything Wrong With Ontario's Priority List
stories.sub: $198 million for a parking lot while transit crumbles is not a vision
stories.summary: The province's commitment of $198 million to a parking garage at Ontario Place encapsulates a governing philosophy that consistently prioritizes automobiles over infrastructure that actually serves people. In a city strained by a global mega-event, this is a remarkable misalignment.
